headline: Myanmar FLOOR CONFIRMATION at 0.0 / Critical — most severe discrete atrocity event since Magway market strike (May 10). Junta airstrike on opposition-run school in Oe Htein Kwin village, Sagaing Region (May 12) killed 22 students and 2 teachers — total 24 dead in a single strike on a clearly civilian-protected site (school). May 7: hospital airstrike in Winmana village, Kani Township, Sagaing. Daily airstrike pattern continues across Sagaing, Chin State, Rakhine State, Karen State. PATTERN INTENSIFICATION: systematic targeting of civilian-protected sites (schools, hospitals, markets) in three consecutive weeks. Floor sustained; conduct documentation appended.

conduct_documentation.summary: May 12 school airstrike (22 students + 2 teachers killed) is the most severe discrete atrocity event since Magway market strike (May 10). Pattern: systematic targeting of civilian-protected sites (schools, hospitals, markets) across three consecutive weeks.
new_conduct_categories_this_cycle: May 12 school airstrike: Junta airstrike on opposition-run school in Oe Htein Kwin village, Sagaing Region. 22 students + 2 teachers killed = 24 dead in single strike on civilian-protected site (school).
May 7 hospital airstrike: Hospital strike in Winmana village, Kani Township, Sagaing — civilian-protected medical site.
Daily airstrike pattern continues across Sagaing, Chin State, Rakhine State, Karen State.
conduct_documentation.civilian_protected_site_pattern: Three consecutive weeks of strikes on protected civilian sites: schools (May 12), hospitals (May 7, May 3 Fangak/South Sudan separate), markets (May 10 Magway). Pattern documented across separate geographic zones (Sagaing, Magway). This is a systematic targeting pattern, not incidental collateral damage.
conduct_documentation.international_legal_context: Timor-Leste universal-jurisdiction prosecution active (per scanner record). HRW 2026 World Report documents ongoing civilian targeting. Cumulative documentation strengthens any future ICC/universal-jurisdiction case.
conduct_documentation.floor_exit_criteria_impact: Three consecutive weeks of protected-civilian-site targeting raises floor-exit evidentiary burden substantially. Floor exit would require cessation of airstrikes on civilian-protected sites AND accountability process for documented atrocities.

key_evidence: May 12 (in-window): Junta airstrike on opposition-run school in Oe Htein Kwin village, Sagaing Region — 22 students + 2 teachers killed (Moemaka).
May 7 (anchor): Hospital airstrike in Winmana village, Kani Township, Sagaing.
Daily airstrike pattern: continues across Sagaing, Chin State, Rakhine State, Karen State (Moemaka casualty tracker).
HRW 2026 World Report Myanmar chapter documents ongoing civilian targeting pattern.
Pattern: systematic targeting of civilian-protected sites (schools, hospitals, markets) across three consecutive weeks.

evidence.source: Moemaka — Daily casualties amid ongoing airstrikes
evidence.finding: May 12 school airstrike (22 students + 2 teachers killed) in Sagaing. Daily airstrike pattern continues.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 unchanged (floor). No detection mechanisms; civilian harm patterns ignored.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 unchanged (floor). School airstrike, hospital airstrike — civilian-protected sites systematically targeted.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 unchanged (floor). Active harm-causing action; no remedial action.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 unchanged (floor). Ethnic and opposition-aligned populations disproportionately targeted.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 unchanged (floor). Sovereignty claim used to legitimize protected-site strikes.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 unchanged (floor). No acknowledgment; no accountability process.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1 unchanged (floor). No systemic reform; structural harm sustained.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 unchanged (floor). Stated military objectives systematically contradict civilian-protection norms.

math_check.validator_check: Floor sustained. School airstrike documented in floor record.

negativeSignals: Junta airstrike on school in Sagaing (22 students + 2 teachers killed); hospital strike May 7; daily airstrike pattern continues; systematic targeting of civilian-protected sites across three consecutive weeks.
sector_context: Southeast Asia / active-conflict cluster. Myanmar pattern of protected-site strikes parallels Russia conduct in active-conflict cluster (different methods, similar bad-faith engagement with civilian-protection norms).
open_actions_resolved: May 12 school airstrike documented in floor record
open_actions_carried: Watch: cumulative protected-site strike pattern for any change
Watch: ICC referral or other international accountability developments
methodology_consistency_check: Floor-entity conduct documentation only. Composite cannot move below floor. Methodologically consistent.
